title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
hook: Fine-tune giant language models on a single GPU without losing accuracy.
promise: You will understand how QLoRA enables affordable, high-quality fine-tuning of large language models.
prerequisite_note: Bring a sense for how neural networks use numbers to represent knowledge and how models are usually tuned.

glossary.plain: Turning precise numbers into rougher, smaller ones to save memory and speed up computation.
A method that fine-tunes only a small part of a model, leaving most weights unchanged.
Storing weights using just 4 bits instead of the usual 16 or 32, drastically reducing memory use.
Compressing already quantized data even further, squeezing out more memory savings.
Small network parts added for fine-tuning, so the main model stays almost untouched.
The amount of computer memory a model needs to run or train.

cards.title: Fine-tune Giant Models, Tiny Hardware
body: See how QLoRA lets you adapt huge language models on a single GPU without losing performance.

cards.title: Why is fine-tuning so hard?
body: Imagine wanting to teach a giant language model a new skill, but your computer cannot even hold it in memory.
Before QLoRA, fine-tuning required expensive, specialized hardware. This locked out most people from adapting large models.

cards.title: How QLoRA unlocks fine-tuning
body: First, QLoRA shrinks the model size by quantizing its weights to just 4 bits. Then, it adds lightweight adapters for learning new tasks.
This combination means you can fine-tune the model efficiently, using far less memory, and still get strong results.
cards.caption: QLoRA's flow: compress, adapt, and fine-tune, all on regular hardware.

cards.title: How does QLoRA compare to classic fine-tuning?
body: Toggle to see how memory and accessibility change when you use QLoRA instead of the traditional approach.
cards.offNote: Needs huge GPUs, high costs, and often cannot fit the model.
cards.onNote: Fits on a single GPU, costs much less, and keeps accuracy nearly the same.

cards.title: The trick: quantize, then adapt
body: QLoRA's key insight is to aggressively compress the model using 4-bit quantization, then fine-tune only tiny adapter layers.
This keeps most of the model frozen and tiny, while still letting it learn new skills accurately with minimal hardware.

cards.title: How does quantization affect performance?
body: Slide to see how dropping bits changes memory use and quality.
cards.low: Huge memory use, perfect accuracy, impossible on most hardware.
cards.mid: 8-bit: Moderate memory, small accuracy drop, possible on good GPUs.
cards.high: Tiny memory, nearly the same accuracy, runs on a single consumer GPU.

cards.title: What goes into QLoRA?
body: Pick the ingredients that make QLoRA efficient and effective.

choices.label: 4-bit quantization
choices.note: Shrinks the model so it fits in memory.

choices.label: LoRA adapters
choices.note: Adds small layers for efficient fine-tuning.

choices.label: Frozen base model
choices.note: Keeps most model weights unchanged during tuning.

choices.label: Single GPU training
choices.note: Enables affordable, accessible fine-tuning for more users.

cards.title: QLoRA’s impact in numbers
body: QLoRA matches or beats full-precision fine-tuning on many benchmarks, while using a fraction of the resources.
It makes adapting large models practical for researchers and startups without massive budgets.

bars.label: Accuracy Loss
bars.note: Less than 1% drop on many tasks

bars.label: Cost Reduction
bars.note: Fine-tuning cost slashed by up to 75%

bars.label: Benchmark Score
bars.note: Matches or exceeds full-precision on common tests

cards.caption: QLoRA delivers huge savings with almost no loss in quality.

cards.title: Why QLoRA changed the game
body: QLoRA democratized fine-tuning, letting more people adapt large language models to new domains or languages.
Its approach inspired a wave of efficient adaptation techniques, making advanced AI more accessible and affordable.

recap.label: Adapt
recap.note: Add and tune small LoRA adapters.

recap.label: Fine-tune
recap.note: Train efficiently on new tasks with tiny hardware.

cards.title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
body: Explore the original paper for technical details and more benchmarks.
